local_names.tamil: வியட்நாமிய பன்றி இறைச்சி சாண்ட்விச் (Bánh Mì Thịt)

aliases: வியட்நாமிய பன்றி இறைச்சி சாண்ட்விச் (Bánh Mì Thịt)

tamil: வியட்நாமிய பன்றி இறைச்சி சாண்ட்விச் (Bánh Mì Thịt)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term specifically for bánh mì; 'sandwich food product' (FOODON_00002473) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Sandwiches) used as closest parent; no specific bánh mì code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for bánh mì sandwich; omitted to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bánh mì sandwich weighing approximately 200 g, comprising a filled French baguette with pork belly, cold cuts, pâté, mayonnaise, pickled vegetables, and fresh herbs.

nutrient_source.title: Bánh mì thịt (Vietnamese pork sandwich) — composite nutrient estimate based on USDA FoodData Central ingredient data: French bread (SR Legacy #18033), pork belly roasted (SR Legacy #10006), pork liver pâté (SR Legacy #07063), mayonnaise (SR Legacy #04025), pickled vegetables, and fresh herbs
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for bánh mì thịt as a composite dish. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data using standard recipe proportions: baguette ~40%, roasted pork belly ~25%, Vietnamese cold cuts ~10%, pork pâté ~5%, mayonnaise ~5%, pickled daikon/carrot ~8%, cucumber ~4%, cilantro/chilli ~3%. Energy calculated by Atwater factors. This is a medium-confidence composite estimate.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of baguette-based sandwiches with mixed fillings — analogous estimate based on French baguette GI (GI=57, University of Sydney GI Database) adjusted downward for protein- and fat-rich fillings (pork, pâté, mayonnaise) which slow gastric emptying and reduce postprandial glucose response
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for bánh mì thịt. French baguette has a published GI of 57 (University of Sydney GI Database). The substantial protein (pork belly, cold cuts, pâté) and fat (mayonnaise, pork fat) content of the filling significantly attenuates the glycemic response of the bread component. A composite GI of approximately 45 is estimated, consistent with published data on mixed meat-and-bread sandwiches. GL per 200 g serving = (45 × 22.5 × 2.0) / 100 = 20.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg/100 g; ~1040 mg per sandwich) from processed cold cuts, pâté, and mayonnaise
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit bánh mì thịt to occasional consumption, as a single sandwich may provide approximately 45–52% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, primarily from cured pork products and condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load from wheat baguette (~45 g per sandwich) with attenuated glycemic response due to protein and fat co-ingestion
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes may consume bánh mì thịt in moderation; the estimated glycemic load of ~20 per sandwich is moderate, and the protein and fat content from pork fillings help blunt postprandial glucose excursions, but portion control and monitoring of blood glucose response are advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~4.1 g/100 g) and sodium (~520 mg/100 g) from roasted pork belly, pâté, and processed cold cuts
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of bánh mì thịt due to its high saturated fat and sodium content from pork belly and processed meats, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol and blood pressure; choosing leaner fillings or reducing portion size is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bánh mì thịt is a traditional Vietnamese pork sandwich served on a crispy French baguette, a culinary legacy of French colonial influence in Vietnam. The sandwich is assembled with a generous spread of homemade mayonnaise and smooth pork liver pâté on the inside of a freshly baked, light-crumbed baguette. It is then layered with sliced roasted pork belly (thịt heo quay), Vietnamese pork cold cuts such as chả lụa (steamed pork roll), chilli sate paste, pickled daikon and carrot (đồ chua), fresh cucumber slices, fragrant cilantro (coriander), and sliced fresh chilli. The result is a complex interplay of textures — crispy crust, tender pork, crunchy pickled vegetables — and flavours — savoury, umami-rich, tangy, herbaceous, and mildly spicy. Nutritionally, bánh mì thịt is a moderate-energy, protein-rich food (~248 kcal and ~11.5 g protein per 100 g) with notable sodium content from the cured and processed pork components. The dish is not suitable for halal or kosher diets due to pork content. Clinically, the high sodium load (~1040 mg per sandwich) is a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load is moderate (~20 per sandwich), making it manageable for people with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in controlled portions. In Singapore, bánh mì is widely available at Vietnamese restaurants and food stalls, reflecting the city-state's diverse Southeast Asian culinary landscape.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Vietnamese pork sandwich?
What is in a traditional banh mi?
Is banh mi high in sodium?
What is the GI of banh mi?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for bánh mì thịt as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data using standard recipe proportions. GI is an analogous estimate based on French baguette GI adjusted for protein and fat content of fillings. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA data is high quality and the recipe proportions are well-established for this dish type.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component data for this composite dish estimate. All other -1 values in per_100g have been replaced with estimated composite values where defensible component data exists. Sodium is notably high (~520 mg/100 g) due to processed pork products (cold cuts, pâté, roasted pork belly). The dish contains pork and is not suitable for halal or kosher diets. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for bánh mì thịt was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date. GL per 200 g serving calculated as (45 × 22.5 × 2.0) / 100 = 20.3 g. The cuisine field in the input JSON was listed as 'Singapore' reflecting the serving context (field survey data); the region_of_origin has been corrected to Vietnam as the dish's authentic origin. The food is served at Co Chung Restaurant, Lorong Telok, Singapore, at SGD 16.48.
